Metadata.Coram: High Court — Originating Summons No 1498 of 2018
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal

Facts.Text: Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal is a practising lawyer in Singapore.
At the time of the application, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was practising at Oxon Law LLC, which is his own law practice where he is the only member and director.
Prior to practising at Oxon Law LLC, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal practised at Camford Law Corporation, a boutique corporate law practice, where he was a director.

As part of his corporate practice, over a period of about 20 years, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal served as resident nominee director for various companies for clients, and at various times had been the resident nominee director for 12 companies.

For two of the companies for which he acted as resident nominee director, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal served as a director for only one day in the course of setting up the companies on an urgent basis for clients.
While employed by Camford Law Corporation, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal agreed to be appointed as a nominee resident director of Innovationz Pte Ltd and was appointed on 2010-08-23.

In his role as nominee resident director of Innovationz Pte Ltd, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was responsible for approving director resolutions related to the filing of annual returns with ACRA and providing assistance usually required of a nominee director, and he did not have day-to-day management roles over the company’s business.

From July 2015 to October 2015, Camford Law Corporation, through S Natarajan, sent multiple written communications to Sand Hill Counsel requesting instructions in relation to Spartan Trading Pte Ltd and Mango Games Pte Ltd, including asking for an action plan to finalise accounts and decide whether to wind up or otherwise deal with the companies.

In December 2015, S Natarajan informed Sand Hill Counsel that recent changes to Singapore law could result in repercussions for Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal if Singapore filing and regulatory requirements for Spartan Trading Pte Ltd and Mango Games Pte Ltd were not complied with, and stated that Camford Law Corporation might inform ACRA that the overseas directors and shareholders were not contactable.
Sand Hill Counsel, a foreign law firm, instructed Camford Law Corporation in relation to Spartan Trading Pte Ltd and Mango Games Pte Ltd.
Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal acted as resident nominee director of Spartan Trading Pte Ltd on the instructions of Camford Law Corporation’s client.
Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal acted as resident nominee director of Mango Games Pte Ltd on the instructions of Camford Law Corporation’s client.

On 2016-09-13, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal sent an email to Sand Hill Counsel stating that it was best to have the client’s concrete action plan for Mango Games Pte Ltd because he did not have options at that stage, and he wished to be replaced as nominee director due to the lack of action to regularise outstanding filings.

In September 2016, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal sent four emails to Sand Hill Counsel explaining that he wished to be replaced as nominee director of Mango Games Pte Ltd because outstanding filings had not been regularised.
On 2017-01-23, S Natarajan informed ACRA that Mango Games Pte Ltd’s board of directors was reviewing plans for the company and would decide whether to revive the business or wind up the company.

Around February 2017, before leaving Camford Law Corporation, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal instructed S Natarajan to follow up with Innovationz Pte Ltd’s executive directors on their statutory filing requirements, including outstanding annual returns.
Around February 2017, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was informed by S Natarajan that the directors of Innovationz Pte Ltd were uncontactable.
Around February 2017, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal left his employment with Camford Law Corporation.
On 2016-10-04, Spartan Trading Pte Ltd was struck off the register by the Registrar of Companies under section 344 of the Companies Act.
On 2017-03-16, Mango Games Pte Ltd was struck off the register by the Registrar of Companies under section 344 of the Companies Act.
On 2017-12-04, Innovationz Pte Ltd was struck off the register by the Registrar of Companies under section 344 of the Companies Act.
Around July 2018, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal attempted to file his prospective appointment as director of a new company using ACRA’s online system, and the filing was rejected by the system.
After the rejection of his attempted director appointment filing around July 2018, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal wrote to ACRA to resolve what he assumed was a technical issue.
On 2018-08-10, ACRA notified Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal by email that he had been disqualified under section 155A of the Companies Act for a period of five years with effect from 2017-12-04.

On 2018-09-05, ACRA issued a letter explaining that Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal had been listed as a director of three companies whose annual returns had not been filed for a number of years and which had been struck off under section 344(1) of the Companies Act; he first saw this letter on 2018-09-17.

The ACRA letter dated 2018-09-05 identified Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, Mango Games Pte Ltd, and Innovationz Pte Ltd as companies for which Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was a director at the time they were struck off, and set out the respective dates of striking off.

On 2018-08-07, a joint application for administrative restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd was submitted to ACRA by Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal together with former directors and members Sriwastawa Sharad Kumar and Bhatia Vinod Kumar under section 344D of the Companies Act.
On 2018-08-08, further joint administrative restoration applications relating to Innovationz Pte Ltd were submitted to ACRA by Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal and the former directors and members.

In the administrative restoration applications submitted on 2018-08-07 and 2018-08-08, the applicants stated that Innovationz Pte Ltd was, at the time of striking off, carrying on business or in operation, as a ground for restoration under Regulation 6 of the Companies (Striking Off) Regulations 2015.

In support of the administrative restoration applications for Innovationz Pte Ltd, documents were submitted to ACRA showing that the company had conducted financial transactions during the financial year 2017.
On 2018-09-13, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal wrote to ACRA to enquire about the status of the restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd.
Following Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal’s enquiry on 2018-09-13, ACRA requested information on who Innovationz Pte Ltd intended to appoint as its locally resident director if it was restored.

After ACRA’s request in September 2018, S Natarajan wrote to ACRA confirming that another local resident director, besides Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal, would be appointed for Innovationz Pte Ltd upon restoration.

On 2018-10-08, ACRA approved the administrative restoration applications of Sriwastawa Sharad Kumar and Bhatia Vinod Kumar, and Innovationz Pte Ltd was restored to the register under section 344E(2) of the Companies Act.
On 2018-10-10, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal wrote to ACRA to enquire about the status of his disqualification under section 155A of the Companies Act.

On 2018-10-12, ACRA informed Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal by email that his disqualification under section 155A would remain in place and stated that restoration of a struck-off company does not change the fact that the company had been struck off.

On 2018-12-06, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal filed an originating summons in the High Court seeking relief under section 344G(3) of the Companies Act to be placed in the same position as director as if Innovationz Pte Ltd had not been struck off, and seeking a declaration that he is not disqualified under section 155A.

On 2018-12-13, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal received a letter from Ramani Baggialakshmi, a current director and shareholder of Innovationz Pte Ltd, stating that Innovationz Pte Ltd had no objections to his application in the originating summons.
By 2018-12-13, Innovationz Pte Ltd’s shareholders had expressed support for Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal to continue as a director of the company following its restoration.

In his application filed on 2018-12-06, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal also sought, in the alternative, leave under section 155A(3) of the Companies Act to continue acting as a director or to be allowed to be a director of several companies, including Oxon Law LLC, in which he had been a director at the time he was informed of his disqualification.

From the date he was notified of his disqualification under section 155A, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal did not act as a director or carry out the roles and responsibilities of a director in respect of the companies for which he sought leave.

Due to his disqualification, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was unable to act as a director of Oxon Law LLC and Oxon Global Pte Ltd, which limited his ability to pitch for legal projects, pursue banking facilities for Oxon Law LLC, and approve compliance-related statutory filings with ACRA and the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore on behalf of Oxon Law LLC.
At the time of the hearing dates in March and April 2019, there were concerns regarding Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal’s ability to renew his practising certificate due to his disqualification status.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, after Innovationz Pte Ltd was restored to the register, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal should be treated for the purposes of section 155A of the Companies Act as if Innovationz Pte Ltd had not been struck off, so that his disqualification from acting as a director would not apply.
Party_Details.Rule: Under section 344G(1) of the Companies Act, when a company’s name is restored to the register under the statutory restoration provisions, the company is to be regarded as having continued in existence as if its name had not been struck off the register. Under section 344G(3), on the application of any person, the court may give directions and make orders it considers just for placing the company and other persons in the same position, as nearly as may be, as if the company had not been dissolved or its name had not been struck off the register. Under section 155A(1) of the Companies Act, a person who was a director of at least three companies that were struck off under section 344 within a specified five-year period is disqualified for five years from acting as a director or taking part in the management of any company, and under section 155A(3) such a person may apply to the court for leave to act as a director or to take part in management despite the disqualification.
Party_Details.Application: Innovationz Pte Ltd, Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, and Mango Games Pte Ltd were struck off the register while Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal was a director of each company, and ACRA treated these events as satisfying the conditions in section 155A(1) for a five-year disqualification. After Innovationz Pte Ltd was restored to the register under section 344E(2) based on evidence that it had been carrying on business at the time of striking off, Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal applied under section 344G(3) for directions to place him in the same position as if Innovationz Pte Ltd had not been struck off, including in relation to his status under section 155A. The court considered the statutory language that a restored company is to be regarded as having continued in existence, the breadth of the power to make just directions to restore parties to their pre-striking-off position, and the factual circumstances of his nominee directorships, his efforts to obtain instructions and information for statutory filings, the communication difficulties with foreign clients and overseas directors, and the practical constraints on his ability to resign or initiate other corporate steps. The court also considered the absence of objections from the companies for which he sought to act as director and the practical impact of the disqualification on his ability to manage his own law practice.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log:
The judge analysed section 344G of the Companies Act as a relatively new provision, noting that section 344G(1) creates a statutory fiction that a restored company is to be regarded as having continued in existence as if its name had not been struck off, and that section 344G(3) confers a broad discretion on the court to give directions and make orders that seem just to place the company and other persons in the same position, as nearly as may be, as if the company had not been struck off. The judge drew on English authorities interpreting the analogous section 1032 of the UK Companies Act 2006, including Joddrell v Peaktone Limited and Davy v Pickering, to emphasise that the discretion is general but must be exercised only for the stated purpose and only if it seems just. The judge rejected the intervener’s submission that the same restrictive test and factors applicable to an application under section 155A(3) should govern an application under section 344G(3), holding that sections 155A and 344G operate in different spheres and that Parliament did not subject one to the other when enacting them together. The judge accepted that section 155A(3) is the general route for a disqualified person to seek leave to act as a director, where the court focuses on the person’s conduct leading to disqualification and future compliance, but found that section 344G(3) specifically addresses the situation where a company has been restored and allows the court to reverse particular consequences of the striking off, including a disqualification under section 155A(1), if that is just. The judge noted that ACRA itself had previously not opposed a similar application in Re Phua Teck Chew, indicating that relief from section 155A disqualification can be sought via the restoration route.

In assessing whether it was just to exercise the discretion in favour of the plaintiff, the judge examined the plaintiff’s conduct in relation to Innovationz Pte Ltd, Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, and Mango Games Pte Ltd. The judge accepted that the plaintiff was a nominee director without day-to-day management roles and that he depended on information and instructions from Camford’s clients and overseas directors to prepare accounts and file annual returns. The judge found that Camford, through S Natarajan, had repeatedly contacted Sand Hill Counsel between July and October 2015 seeking instructions and an action plan for Spartan Trading and Mango Games, had warned of possible repercussions under Singapore law, and had indicated that ACRA might be informed that overseas directors and shareholders were not contactable. The judge noted that there was no substantive response and that Sand Hill Counsel itself had difficulty contacting the ultimate clients. The judge accepted that the plaintiff had written multiple times in September 2016 seeking to be replaced as nominee director of Mango Games Pte Ltd and that he was constrained by the statutory requirement for at least one locally resident director, which prevented him from resigning without a replacement.

The judge considered the intervener’s criticisms that the plaintiff had not ensured filing of annual returns and had not taken additional measures such as insisting on contractual conditions, additional resident directors, or shareholdings to enable winding up. The judge regarded many of these suggested measures as unrealistic in the context of corporate practice and noted that clients would likely resist such conditions. The judge also observed that seeking court orders for access to documents would be of limited practical value where other directors were uncontactable or overseas. The judge found no evidence that the plaintiff could have foreseen, at the time of accepting the appointments, that the other directors would become uncontactable, and noted that communication with the clients was handled primarily by S Natarajan as relationship partner, especially after the plaintiff left Camford.

The judge accepted that ACRA had restored Innovationz Pte Ltd on the basis that it was carrying on business at the time of striking off, and reasoned that, had this information been available earlier, the Registrar would not have had grounds under the Companies (Striking Off) Regulations 2015 to strike off the company. The judge concluded that, in light of the statutory fiction in section 344G(1), it was prima facie consistent with the purpose of restoration to place the plaintiff in the position he would have been in had Innovationz Pte Ltd not been struck off, subject to the justice of doing so. The judge considered the protective purposes of the disqualification regime discussed in Ong Chow Hong v Public Prosecutor, distinguishing between specific protection against a particular director and general deterrence. The judge found that the plaintiff’s conduct did not amount to serious lapses in corporate behaviour of the type envisaged in that case, that his situation as a nominee director appointed before the introduction of section 155A was relatively unusual, and that maintaining his disqualification would not significantly advance the general deterrent purpose. The judge also noted the absence of objections from the companies for which he sought to act as director and the significant hardship caused by his inability to act as director of his own law practice and related entities.

Balancing these considerations, the judge held that it was just to exercise the discretion under section 344G(3) to direct that the plaintiff be placed in the same position as director as if Innovationz Pte Ltd had never been struck off and to make a consequential declaration that he is not disqualified under section 155A. Having granted this relief, the judge found it unnecessary to determine the plaintiff’s alternative application under section 155A(3). The court therefore allowed the application in terms of the primary prayers and treated the plaintiff as not disqualified under section 155A(1) for the relevant period.

Metadata.Coram: High Court — Originating Summons No 1498 of 2018
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Innovationz Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Innovationz Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore.
From 2010-08-23, Innovationz Pte Ltd had Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal as its only resident director.
Innovationz Pte Ltd had executive directors who were responsible for the company’s business operations, separate from the resident nominee director role of Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal.
The corporate secretarial functions of Innovationz Pte Ltd were handled by the company secretary, S Natarajan, a lawyer and director at Camford Law Corporation.
A corporate secretarial executive employed at Camford Law Corporation carried out filings and lodgements with ACRA for Innovationz Pte Ltd and reported to S Natarajan.
By around February 2017, Innovationz Pte Ltd had not filed its annual returns for a number of years.
Around February 2017, the executive directors of Innovationz Pte Ltd were not contactable by S Natarajan when he attempted to follow up on statutory filing requirements.

On 2017-12-04, the Registrar of Companies struck off Innovationz Pte Ltd from the register under section 344 of the Companies Act on the basis that the company was believed not to be carrying on business or in operation.

On 2018-08-07 and 2018-08-08, former directors and members of Innovationz Pte Ltd, including Sriwastawa Sharad Kumar and Bhatia Vinod Kumar, together with Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal, submitted applications to ACRA for administrative restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd under section 344D of the Companies Act.

In the administrative restoration applications submitted in August 2018, the applicants stated that Innovationz Pte Ltd was carrying on business or in operation at the time it was struck off, as a ground for restoration under Regulation 6 of the Companies (Striking Off) Regulations 2015.
During the financial year 2017, Innovationz Pte Ltd conducted financial transactions, indicating ongoing business activity.
Documents submitted to ACRA in support of the restoration applications for Innovationz Pte Ltd included records of financial activity in 2017 to the company remained a going concern.
On 2018-09-13, ACRA requested information on who Innovationz Pte Ltd intended to appoint as its locally resident director if the company was restored.
Following ACRA’s request in September 2018, S Natarajan informed ACRA that Innovationz Pte Ltd would appoint another local resident director besides Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal upon restoration.

On 2018-10-08, ACRA approved the administrative restoration applications of Sriwastawa Sharad Kumar and Bhatia Vinod Kumar, and Innovationz Pte Ltd was restored to the register under section 344E(2) of the Companies Act.

On 2018-12-13, Innovationz Pte Ltd’s director and shareholder, Ramani Baggialakshmi, sent a letter stating that Innovationz Pte Ltd had no objections to Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal’s application in the originating summons.
By 2018-12-13, Ramani Baggialakshmi was a director and shareholder of Innovationz Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, following the restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd to the register, the legal position of Innovationz Pte Ltd and persons connected with it, including its former resident director, should be adjusted so that they are treated as if the company had not been struck off.
Party_Details.Rule: When a company’s name is restored to the register under the Companies Act restoration provisions, the company is to be regarded as having continued in existence as if its name had not been struck off the register, and the court may, on application, give directions and make orders it considers just to place the company and other persons in the same position, as nearly as may be, as if the company had not been dissolved or struck off.
Party_Details.Application: Innovationz Pte Ltd was struck off the register in December 2017 after a period in which annual returns had not been filed and its directors were not contactable. In August 2018, former directors and members, together with the former resident director, applied for administrative restoration, stating that the company had been carrying on business at the time of striking off and providing documents showing financial transactions in 2017. ACRA accepted these materials and restored Innovationz Pte Ltd to the register in October 2018 under section 344E(2). The restoration triggered the statutory provision that the company is to be regarded as having continued in existence, and it created a context in which the court could consider whether to make further directions to align the positions of the company and related persons with the situation that would have existed if the company had not been struck off.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge treated the restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd under section 344E(2) as establishing that the statutory conditions for restoration were met, including that the company had been carrying on business at the time of striking off. The judge reasoned that, under section 344G(1), the effect of restoration is that Innovationz Pte Ltd is deemed to have continued in existence as if it had not been struck off, and that section 344G(3) empowers the court to make directions to place the company and other persons in the pre-striking-off position where that seems just. The judge inferred that, had ACRA been aware of the company’s ongoing business activity before the striking-off decision, the Registrar would not have had grounds to strike off the company under the Companies (Striking Off) Regulations 2015. This reasoning supported the view that it was consistent with the statutory purpose of restoration to adjust the position of the company’s former resident director so that his status would reflect the assumption that the company had not been struck off. The judge did not make separate liability findings against Innovationz Pte Ltd, as the focus of the proceedings was on the plaintiff’s status and the interaction between the restoration regime and the director disqualification regime.

Metadata.Coram: High Court — Originating Summons No 1498 of 2018
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority

Facts.Text: The Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) is the corporate regulator in Singapore responsible for maintaining the register of companies and enforcing filing and regulatory requirements under the Companies Act.

On 2016-10-04, ACRA, through the Registrar of Companies, struck off Spartan Trading Pte Ltd from the register under section 344 of the Companies Act after annual returns had not been filed for three years.

On 2017-03-16, ACRA, through the Registrar of Companies, struck off Mango Games Pte Ltd from the register under section 344 of the Companies Act after annual returns had not been filed for four years.

On 2017-12-04, ACRA, through the Registrar of Companies, struck off Innovationz Pte Ltd from the register under section 344 of the Companies Act after its annual returns had not been filed for several years.

On 2017-01-26, ACRA informed Camford Law Corporation that a director of Mango Games Pte Ltd had to lodge an objection against the proposed striking off if the company wished to avoid being struck off.

On 2018-08-10, ACRA sent an email to Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal notifying him that he had been disqualified under section 155A of the Companies Act for a period of five years with effect from 2017-12-04, based on his directorships in Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, Mango Games Pte Ltd, and Innovationz Pte Ltd at the times they were struck off.

On 2018-09-05, ACRA issued a letter explaining that Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal had been listed as a director of three companies whose annual returns had not been filed for a number of years and which had been struck off under section 344(1) of the Companies Act, and that this triggered section 155A(1).
In the 2018-09-05 letter, ACRA identified Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, Mango Games Pte Ltd, and Innovationz Pte Ltd as the companies Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal’s disqualification under section 155A(1).

After receiving an enquiry from Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal on 2018-09-13 about the restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd, ACRA requested details of the intended locally resident director for the company if it was restored.

On 2018-10-08, ACRA approved the administrative restoration applications of former directors and members of Innovationz Pte Ltd and restored the company to the register under section 344E(2) of the Companies Act.

On 2018-10-12, ACRA informed Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal by email that the successful restoration of a company’s name to the register under section 344(5) or section 344E does not affect a person disqualified under section 155A(1), and that such a person remains disqualified because restoration does not change the fact that the company had been struck off.
After being served with a copy of the originating summons filed by Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal, ACRA applied for and obtained leave to intervene in the proceedings to object to his application.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the regulatory consequences of striking off three companies, including the operation of section 155A(1) of the Companies Act, should continue to apply to Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal after Innovationz Pte Ltd was restored to the register, or whether the court should adjust his position under section 344G(3).
Party_Details.Rule: Under section 344 of the Companies Act, the Registrar may strike a company off the register if there is reasonable cause to believe that the company is not carrying on business or is not in operation, following prescribed notice procedures. Under section 155A(1), a person who was a director of at least three companies that were struck off under section 344 within a specified five-year period is disqualified for five years from acting as a director or taking part in the management of any company. Under section 344D and section 344E, former directors or members may apply for administrative restoration of a struck-off company, and if restoration is granted, the company is treated as having continued in existence, with the court empowered under section 344G(3) to make just directions to restore the positions of affected persons.
Party_Details.Application: ACRA applied section 344 to strike off Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, Mango Games Pte Ltd, and Innovationz Pte Ltd after periods of non-filing of annual returns and apparent inactivity, and then applied section 155A(1) to disqualify Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal for five years based on his directorships in those companies at the times of striking off. When former directors and members later applied for administrative restoration of Innovationz Pte Ltd, ACRA processed the applications under section 344D and section 344E, requested information about the intended locally resident director, and restored the company after receiving documents showing business activity in 2017. After restoration, ACRA informed Bijynath s/o Ram Nawal that, in its view, restoration did not affect his disqualification under section 155A(1) because the fact of the prior striking off remained. In the court proceedings, ACRA, as intervener, maintained that section 344G(3) should not be used to undo the disqualification except in circumstances equivalent to those justifying relief under section 155A(3), and argued that the plaintiff’s conduct in relation to statutory filings and supervision of the companies did not warrant such relief.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge recorded that ACRA had struck off Spartan Trading Pte Ltd, Mango Games Pte Ltd, and Innovationz Pte Ltd under section 344 after periods of non-filing of annual returns and that ACRA had then treated these events as satisfying the conditions for disqualification under section 155A(1). The judge noted that ACRA restored Innovationz Pte Ltd under section 344E(2) after being presented with evidence of business activity in 2017, but that ACRA maintained the position that restoration did not affect the plaintiff’s disqualification. As intervener, ACRA argued that section 344G(3) should be interpreted narrowly so that the same stringent criteria applied under section 155A(3) would govern any attempt to remove a disqualification via the restoration route, and that otherwise section 344G(3) would provide a backdoor to avoid the consequences of section 155A. ACRA also advanced policy arguments based on the protective and deterrent purposes of the disqualification regime, relying on prior case law and unreported decisions. The judge rejected ACRA’s proposed limitation on section 344G(3), holding that the statutory language did not support importing the section 155A(3) test into section 344G(3), and that Parliament had allowed both provisions to coexist without cross-qualification. The judge accepted that ACRA’s restoration decision implied that the statutory grounds for striking off Innovationz Pte Ltd would not have been met had the business activity been known earlier, and used this as part of the reasoning to justify adjusting the plaintiff’s position under section 344G(3). The judge ultimately did not adopt ACRA’s view that the plaintiff’s disqualification should remain in place and instead exercised the discretion under section 344G(3) to relieve the plaintiff from the disqualification, while acknowledging ACRA’s role and submissions in the proceedings.
